run_metadata.coverage_summary: DEGRADED RUN (egress), FOURTH consecutive day. Every WebFetch/direct-curl was again blocked by an organization egress-policy HTTP 403 on the CONNECT tunnel - reproduced this run against tenders.gov.au via the WebFetch tool (403 Forbidden) and via direct curl through the proxy (curl 56, 'CONNECT tunnel failed, response 403'); the proxy status endpoint shows recentRelayFailures empty, confirming a policy denial (per /root/.ccr/README.md a blanket 403 must not be retried or routed around). Consequently NO live tender detail page or specification document could be opened this run, so every field below is derived from public WebSearch result snippets (indexed summaries of the AusTender/buy.nsw/VendorPanel detail pages), NOT from a direct page fetch, and must be re-verified once egress is restored. Despite the block, WebSearch coverage this run was broad and productive: Tier 1 was swept across all nine jurisdictions (Commonwealth AusTender, NSW buy.nsw, VIC Buying-for-Victoria, QLD QTenders, WA Tenders WA, SA Tenders SA, TAS, NT QTOL, ACT) and yielded eleven currently-open opportunities with real IDs/titles/buyers/closing dates; Tier 2 (local government via VendorPanel, GBE water corporations, universities, NSW Health) was sampled; and the mandatory Tier 3 (SAP Ariba Discovery, EstimateOne, corporate buyer portals) was attempted and confirmed registration/login-gated with no publicly-visible AU RFx capturable without an authenticated session. Net-new sources discovered this run: Brisbane City Council SAP Ariba supplier portal, Sydney Water eTendering sub-portal (buy.nsw), and University of Sydney Suppliers/UniBuy procurement. Corporate portals (BHP/Rio/Fortescue on Ariba; Qantas/Westpac on Coupa; Woolworths/Coles) remain registration- or invitation-gated and are recorded as portals only, never fabricated as tenders.
gaps: EGRESS POLICY BLOCK (dominant gap, now a 4-DAY pattern unchanged from 2026-06-29/30 and 2026-07-01): the session egress proxy returned HTTP 403 on the CONNECT tunnel for ALL destinations, confirmed this run for tenders.gov.au via the WebFetch tool (HTTP 403 Forbidden) and reproduced with a direct curl (exit 56, 'CONNECT tunnel failed, response 403'); proxy /__agentproxy/status shows recentRelayFailures empty. Per /root/.ccr/README.md this is an organization egress-policy denial and was not retried or routed around. No tender detail page or specification document could be opened. ACTION REQUIRED: add AU government/buyer domains to the session egress allowlist (e.g. *.gov.au, *.nsw.gov.au, vendorpanel.com.au, qtenders.hpw.qld.gov.au, *.ariba.com, *.coupahost.com) so WebFetch can read full deliverables/requirements/documents again.

ALL eleven captured opportunities are SNIPPET-DERIVED from public WebSearch summaries of buyer-originated detail pages (AusTender, buy.nsw, VendorPanel), NOT from a direct page fetch. For each, the title, buyer, procurement type and closing date were extractable, and in most cases a short description; but the tender documents, full scope-of-work, evaluation criteria, value, eligibility, requirements and mandatory conditions could NOT be captured because the fetch was blocked. Every entry is flagged entries_pending_detail and needs a deliverables/detail verification pass once egress is restored.

CONFLICTING closing date for the NSW DCJ 'video recording solutions for 25 YJ transport vehicles' opportunity: one search snippet reported closing 7-Jul-2026 and another 28-Jul-2026. Recorded as 2026-07-07 with the discrepancy noted; must be verified on the buy.nsw detail page once egress is restored.

University EOIs (University of Sydney Modern Methods of Construction Panel and Electrical Test-and-Tag; University of Melbourne Refurbishment Contractor Panel; Monash Moodle Managed Services and cardiovascular equipment) surfaced only via third-party AGGREGATOR snippets (australiantenders.com.au, globaltenders.com) with no IDs or closing dates and could not be confirmed on the buyer's own portal (fetch blocked). Per the buyer-originated rule these were treated as a discovery signal only and NOT captured as confirmed tenders; the University of Sydney own procurement portal was instead recorded as a discovered source. Verify on the universities' own portals next run.

Puckapunyal Building Refurbishment and North & East Bandiana Safety Works (both Dept of Defence - DSRG, closing 3-Jul-2026) were captured from AusTender snippets but their ATM IDs were not extractable from search; dedup keys are title-based and IDs need to be filled on a fetch-enabled run.

Tenders WA (PROPTA-style ids), Tenders SA/Buying4SA, Tenders Tasmania, NT QTOL and Tenders ACT: only portal/landing pages were indexed this run; no individual current opportunity was extractable via search (JavaScript-rendered result lists). Deferred to a future run with fetch access. A scheduled Tenders WA outage was noted (Wed 1-Jul-2026 17:00-19:00).

Buying for Victoria: only landing pages plus one closing-date-only snippet (closing 3:30pm Wed 8-Jul-2026, no title/buyer) were indexed; no net-new VIC opportunity was captured this run. Melbourne Water publishes via Buying for Victoria and was not separately capturable.

Tier 3 (mandatory): SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network Discovery confirmed (via SAP Help Portal) to require a free seller account to view/respond, with public posting dependent on buyer configuration - no publicly-visible AU RFx capturable without login. EstimateOne (construction subcontractor packages) requires registration. Corporate buyer portals (BHP/Rio/Fortescue/Woodside on Ariba; Qantas/Westpac on Coupa; banks on SAP Business Network; Woolworths/Coles) remain registration- or invitation-gated. Portals retained/added in the known source set; no corporate opportunity fabricated. Invitation-only consultancy RFPs remain out of scope.
recommended_seed_additions: Brisbane City Council - SAP Ariba supplier portal (supplierportal.brisbane.qld.gov.au + brisbane.qld.gov.au/business/.../current-tenders). Buyer-originated corporate-platform (SAP Ariba/Business Network) local-government source; a useful seed to find sibling councils that publish via Ariba rather than VendorPanel. Check weekly. (Added to discovered_sources this run.)

Sydney Water - eTendering sub-portal on buy.nsw (tenders.nsw.gov.au/sydneywater) and current-tenders page; a Government Business Enterprise that publishes major EOIs (e.g. Drinking Water Program EPCM, ref SWC-WS5192918179). Add to the GBE sweep list; check weekly. (Added to discovered_sources this run.)

University of Sydney - Suppliers / UniBuy procurement portal (sydney.edu.au/about-us/working-with-the-university/suppliers.html); publishes its own EOIs/panels. Useful as a seed to find sibling universities on the same procurement platform. Check weekly. (Added to discovered_sources this run.)

NEW federal Australian Government Supplier Portal is being rolled out from mid-2026 as a publicly searchable supplier-registration database that complements (does not replace) AusTender - watch for its live URL and add it to Tier 1 once launched.

INFRASTRUCTURE (repeat, now URGENT - 4-day outage): add AU government/buyer domains to the session egress allowlist so WebFetch can open detail pages and documents again. Without this the routine can only ever capture snippet-level data and cannot extract deliverables, documents, evaluation criteria or values.

discovered_sources.source_name: Brisbane City Council - SAP Ariba Supplier Portal
discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch for SAP Ariba Discovery AU public RFx surfaced Brisbane City Council's supplier portal support page; the Council publishes all public tenders on its current-tenders page and alerts suppliers via SAP Ariba / SAP Business Network (supplierportal.brisbane.qld.gov.au).
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated local-government source that uses SAP Ariba rather than VendorPanel. Public tenders are viewable on the current-tenders page; responding requires a free SAP Business Network account. A useful seed to find sibling councils publishing via Ariba.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch for water-corporation procurement surfaced Sydney Water's dedicated eTendering sub-portal on buy.nsw and its own current-tenders page; found an open EPCM EOI (SWC-WS5192918179).
discovered_sources.notes: Government Business Enterprise publishing major EOIs/RFTs via a buy.nsw sub-portal and its own current-tenders page. The NSW eTendering RSS feed (rss.xml) is a candidate machine-readable feed to confirm once egress is restored.

discovered_sources.source_name: University of Sydney - Suppliers / UniBuy Procurement
discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch for Australian university procurement surfaced the University of Sydney's own suppliers page (with EOIs for a Modern Methods of Construction Panel and Electrical Test-and-Tag services referenced via aggregators); recorded the buyer-originated portal rather than the aggregator listings.
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated university procurement portal (UniBuy). Specific current EOIs were only visible via third-party aggregators this run and could not be confirmed on the buyer's own portal (egress 403); verify on the University's own portal next run. Useful seed for sibling universities.

discovered_sources.source_name: SAP Business Network Discovery (SAP Ariba Discovery)
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Mandatory Tier-3 check. SAP Help Portal confirms Business Opportunities on SAP Business Network Discovery require a free seller account to view/respond, with public posting dependent on buyer configuration.
discovered_sources.notes: Largest single corporate opportunity channel. No publicly-visible AU RFx capturable without an authenticated seller account this run; recorded as a portal only. Register a free seller account so a future authenticated run can read open RFx.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Mandatory Tier-3 construction check. E1 lists live commercial construction tenders where Tier-1 builders (Lendlease, Multiplex, John Holland) upload ITT/RFQ subcontractor work packages with drawings and scope of works.
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer(builder)-originated subcontractor work-package channel. Individual packages and documents require a (free) supplier registration; no package capturable without login this run.
